inputs.fcf_source: FY2025 adjusted after-tax income $4044.0M ÷ 570.35M shares = $7.09/sh; excludes investment marks, Fortitude noise, and Corebridge fair-value swings
inputs.normalization_note: Lawrence base uses adjusted after-tax income (General Insurance underwriting plus investment spread), not GAAP net income ($3.1B) which includes realized gains and mark volatility

bear.notes: Cat-heavy year, casualty social inflation accelerates, buybacks pause; multiple compresses to ~9× normalized owner cash

base.notes: Combined ratio near 90%, investment yields hold, repurchases continue; 10× exit on year-10 owner cash

bull.notes: Rate adequacy persists, share count shrinks faster, Corebridge dividends stable; sustained repurchases

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Investment portfolio marked to fair value; adjusted book value $78.02/sh excludes Investments AOCI (10-K FY2025)

option_scan.question: Undeveloped reserves / dormant assets?
option_scan.evidence: Operating global insurer; no land or royalty reserves post-Corebridge spin

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: Global Personal combined ratio 99.0% near breakeven; commercial segments profitable (10-K segment table)

option_scan.question: Backlog / contracted revenue not in FCF path?
option_scan.evidence: Unearned premium reserve $12.9B and renewal book embedded in premium growth assumptions

option_scan.question: Private or illiquid stakes below fair value?
option_scan.evidence: Residual Corebridge stake $1.5B at December 31, 2025; separate overlap key corebridge_and_float_surplus

option_scan.question: Transitory distribution / legal recovery?
option_scan.evidence: Earnings from underwriting and recurring investment income, not litigation recovery

option_scan.question: Embedded product option already in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: Commercial and personal premiums already in consolidated General Insurance revenues $23.7B FY2025

growth_explanation.mechanism: Post-Corebridge General Insurance franchise compounds through rate-adequate commercial lines, investment float at higher yields, and aggressive share repurchases (538M shares outstanding vs 688M in 2023).
growth_explanation.source: AIG/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260212_rpt20251231_acc0000005272_26_000023.htm General Insurance segment table and non-GAAP reconciliation
growth_explanation.bear_falsifier: General Insurance combined ratio exceeds 95% for two consecutive years with no offsetting investment income
growth_explanation.bull_falsifier: Adjusted after-tax income grows faster than net premiums written for four consecutive quarters while combined ratio stays below 88%

component_valuation.coverage_statement: Five additive components map North America Commercial, International Commercial, Global Personal underwriting engines, Corebridge stake and float surplus, and catastrophe/reserve stress reserve once each.

components.label: North America Commercial underwriting and investment spread

valuation.evidence: Primary bridge from FY2025 10-K: adjusted after-tax income $4044.0M ($7.09/sh), adjusted book $78.02/sh, GI net investment income $3433.0M, total debt $9191.0M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.
valuation.cross_check: Reconcile to FY2025 10-K and Q1 2026 10-Q before decision use.
valuation.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

inputs.label: North America Commercial underwriting income FY2025

source.locator: North America Commercial underwriting income $1144.0M; combined ratio 86.8% FY2025

inputs.label: Adjusted after-tax income attributable to AIG common shareholders FY2025

source.locator: Adjusted after-tax income $4044.0M FY2025 (non-GAAP reconciliation, excludes marks and Fortitude noise)

assumptions.label: Normalized adjusted after-tax income allocated to North America Commercial
assumptions.rationale: Largest U.S. commercial franchise; combined ratio improved in 2025.

assumptions.label: Growth years 1–5 on segment owner cash
assumptions.rationale: Mature GI franchise; modest premium growth and buybacks.

assumptions.label: Growth years 6–7 on segment owner cash
assumptions.rationale: Fade as rate cycle normalizes and share count shrinks.

assumptions.label: Required return on segment owner cash
assumptions.rationale: Property-cat, casualty social inflation, and investment-spread risk premium.

assumptions.label: Selling multiple in year 7
assumptions.rationale: Global commercial insurer peer multiples on normalized owner cash.

assumptions.label: Component schedule reconciliation factor
assumptions.rationale: Preserves additive component schedule while filing facts anchor owner-cash bridge.

assumptions.label: Corebridge residual stake plus investment float surplus above segment owner-cash capitalization
assumptions.rationale: Corebridge stake marked on balance sheet; modest float surplus for GI investment income not fully captured in segment DCF; not double-counted in segment engines.

valuation.evidence: Primary bridge from FY2025 10-K: adjusted after-tax income $4044.0M ($7.09/sh), adjusted book $78.02/sh, GI net investment income $3433.0M, total debt $9191.0M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.

source.locator: Liability for unpaid losses and loss adjustment expenses $41,665M FY2025
General Insurance combined ratio 90.1% FY2025 (91.8% prior year)

assumptions.label: Catastrophe, social inflation, Fortitude runoff, and soft-market cycle stress reserve
assumptions.rationale: Negative reserve for $920M FY2025 cat charges, casualty social inflation, and $9.2B debt servicing; not full $41.7B net reserves double-count.

ownership_waterfall.net_economic_claim: One AIG diluted share equals pro-rata North America Commercial, International Commercial, and Global Personal underwriting engines, Corebridge stake and float surplus, less catastrophe and reserve stress.
excluded_claims: General Insurance net investment income largely allocated through adjusted after-tax income in segment DCFs.
Full $41.7B loss reserves are not subtracted twice; catastrophe reserve captures stress claims only.
Adjusted book value ($78.02/sh) is cross-check, not dhando floor; economic value lives in normalized owner cash.
ownership_waterfall.reconciliation: FY2025 adjusted after-tax income $4044.0M on 570.35M shares ($7.09/sh); adjusted book value $78.02/sh; total debt $9191.0M; Corebridge stake $1512.0M.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of AIG, including North America Commercial, International Commercial, and Global Personal underwriting engines, Corebridge stake and float surplus, and catastrophe/reserve stress reserve.
economic_claim.unit_source: FY2025 weighted average diluted shares 570.35M per AIG/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260212_rpt20251231_acc0000005272_26_000023.htm.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating segments valued through owner-cash discount paths on allocated adjusted after-tax income; Corebridge/float surplus and stress reserve are separate overlap keys.

economic_value.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K: book value per share $76.44; adjusted book value $78.02; adjusted after-tax income $4044.0M; economic value in normalized underwriting and investment spread.

component_groups.label: North America Commercial underwriting engine
component_groups.economic_claim: U.S. commercial property, casualty, and specialty platform
component_groups.valuation_basis: Owner-cash discount on North America share of adjusted after-tax income.
component_groups.adjustments: Combined ratio 86.8% FY2025; $478M catastrophe charges in segment.

component_groups.label: International Commercial underwriting engine
component_groups.valuation_basis: Owner-cash discount on International share of adjusted after-tax income.
component_groups.adjustments: Combined ratio 86.9% FY2025; largest cat exposure outside U.S. wildfires.

component_groups.label: Global Personal underwriting engine
component_groups.valuation_basis: Owner-cash discount on Global Personal share of adjusted after-tax income.

component_groups.label: Corebridge stake and float surplus
component_groups.economic_claim: Marked Corebridge residual stake plus float economics above segment DCF
component_groups.valuation_basis: Net asset value on Corebridge interest and modest float surplus.
component_groups.adjustments: Corebridge stake $1.5B; GI net investment income $3.4B.

component_groups.label: Catastrophe and reserve stress reserve
component_groups.economic_claim: Property cat, social inflation, Fortitude runoff, and debt-service reserve
component_groups.valuation_basis: Bounded negative reserve; not full loss-reserve NAV subtraction.
component_groups.adjustments: Total net reserves $41.7B; total debt $9.2B; reserve captures stress not base case.

limitations: Segment splits use underwriting-income-proportional adjusted after-tax income allocation.
Global Personal wildfire and California regulatory risk remain widest judgment band.
